name: Western Union
description_short: Western Sun Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Oklahoma with 6 branches and access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide.
description_long:
Western Sun Federal Credit Union (WSFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma. The institution operates as a federally chartered credit union regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), serving members across northeastern Oklahoma including Tulsa, Bixby, Okmulgee, and Owasso. The organization has been established as a community-focused financial institution providing traditional credit union services to its membership base.

WESFCU offers a comprehensive range of credit union services including share accounts (savings), share certificates (CDs), loan products, and account access through multiple channels. Members can conduct business through six physical branch locations, benefit from on-site ATMs at four branches, and leverage access to 5,600 shared branch and service centers nationwide through the CO-OP network. The credit union also provides surcharge-free access to 30,000 ATMs nationwide, enabling members to withdraw cash without fees at partner locations across the country.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its dual-branch model offering both lobby and drive-through service at most locations, with extended Saturday hours (9am-12pm) at select branches. The Downtown Tulsa location operates with earlier morning hours (8:30am start), accommodating downtown workers. WSFCU's participation in nationwide shared branching and ATM networks provides members with significantly broader access than their six physical locations alone suggest, a key advantage for members who travel or relocate within the United States.

WESFCU operates as a traditional community credit union focused on its regional membership base in Oklahoma. While the website provides clear branch/ATM locator information and service center details, it does not prominently detail specific loan products, rates, membership eligibility criteria, or comprehensive product offerings. The institution appears positioned as a stable, member-focused alternative to traditional banks but lacks extensive online product marketing or digital-first service emphasis evident on the website.
address: Dollar General, 7120 Brecksville Rd, Independence, OH 44131

company_info.headquarters: Dollar General, 7120 Brecksville Rd, Independence, OH 44131

pros: Access to 30,000 surcharge-free CO-OP ATMs nationwide, eliminating out-of-network ATM fees
6 physical branches with extended Saturday drive-through hours (9am-12pm) at most locations
Member access to 5,600 shared branch and service centers nationwide for deposits and withdrawals
On-site ATMs at Bixby, Broken Arrow, Okmulgee, and Owasso branches for 24/7 cash access
Extended drive-through hours (7:30am-5:30pm) Monday-Friday at four locations for working members
Downtown Tulsa branch with early morning lobby hours (8:30am) for downtown professionals
Not-for-profit, member-owned structure prioritizing member benefit over shareholder returns
cons: Only 6 branch locations, all concentrated in the Tulsa, Oklahoma metropolitan area and surrounding region
Two branches (Downtown Tulsa and West Tulsa) lack on-site ATMs and drive-through service
Website does not display current rates, APYs, loan terms, or specific product details to help members compare offerings
No apparent online account opening or digital onboarding process information provided on website
Limited geographic accessibility for members outside of northeastern Oklahoma without relying on shared branching
best_for: Oklahoma residents in the Tulsa metropolitan area seeking a community-focused credit union alternative
Members who value access to nationwide ATM networks and shared branching for travel or relocation flexibility
Consumers preferring member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over traditional for-profit banks
Working professionals needing extended drive-through hours or early morning lobby access
diagnosis: Western Sun Federal Credit Union is best for Oklahoma residents in the Tulsa area seeking community credit union services with strong nationwide ATM and shared branching access. The primary limitation is geographic concentration—members outside northeastern Oklahoma will need to rely on shared branching networks rather than physical branches, and the website lacks product rate and eligibility information necessary for comprehensive evaluation.
services: Loan products (specific types not detailed on branch locator page)
Access to 30,000 surcharge-free CO-OP ATMs nationwide
Shared branch services at 5,600 locations nationwide
Account login and online account access
